viable_jobs.title: Enterprise Analytics Intern - Business Data & Analytics 21
viable_jobs.description:
Be Here. Be Great. Working for a leader in the insurance industry means opportunity for you. Great American Insurance Group's member companies are subsidiaries of American Financial Group. We combine a "small company" culture where your ideas will be heard with "big company" expertise to helpyou succeed. With over 30 specialty and property and casualty operations, there are always opportunities here to learn and grow.

At Great American, we value and recognize the benefits derived when people with different backgrounds and experiences work together to achieve business results. Our goal is to create a workplace where all employees feel included, empowered, and enabled to perform at their best.

We are seeking an Intern to support our Enterprise Analytics team in transforming data into meaningful insights that drive business decisions. This role provides hands-on experience working with enterprise data, analytics tools, and cross-functional partners to deliver clear, reliable, and actionable reporting for business leaders.

Essential Job Functions and Responsibilities
• Support the Enterprise Analytics team in developing, enhancing, and maintaining enterprise-level reporting and dashboards
• Help test and validate reporting solutions to ensure accuracy, consistency, and production readiness
• Collaborate with analytics, technology, and business partners to understand data requirements and translate them into analytic solutions
• Document processes and procedures necessary to support our products
• Apply best practices related to data quality, documentation, and analytics standards
• Build foundational business and data domain knowledge across enterprise functions
• Independently complete assigned tasks while receiving guidance and mentorship from the analytics team
• Perform other duties as assigned in support of enterprise analytics initiatives
Job Requirements
• Field of Study: Data Analytics, Business Analytics, Data Science, Finance, Information Systems, or a related discipline
• Available to work full time during Summer 2026, beginning May 18th
Preferred Experience
• Exposure to data visualization or reporting tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, or similar)
• Basic understanding of data analysis concepts and metrics
• Experience working with structured data sets or databases
• Introductory SQL or querying experience
• Familiarity with gathering requirements and collaborating with technical and business stakeholders
• Demonstrated skills in problem solving, attention to detail, accuracy, and time management
• Strong communication skills and an interest in enterprise-level analytics and decision support
Business Unit:
Business Data and Analytics

Benefits:

We offer competitive benefits packages for full-time and part-time employees*. Full-time employees have access to medical, dental, and vision coverage, wellness plans, parental leave, adoption assistance, and tuition reimbursement. Full-time and eligible part-time employees also enjoy Paid Time Off and paid holidays, a 401(k) plan with company match, an employee stock purchase plan, and commuter benefits.

Compensation varies by role, level, and location and is influenced by skills, experience, and business needs. Your recruiter will provide details about benefits and specific compensation ranges during the hiring process. Learn more at
• Excludes seasonal employees and interns.

viable_jobs.title: 2026 IDS Summer Intern – Data Governance
viable_jobs.description:
:::Please note: This is a temporary contractor opportunity at Novartis :::

Novartis touches the lives of a tenth of the world’s population. Every role here, regardless of contract type, makes an impact on human life!

Novartis US partners with external managed service provider Magnit Global to offer engaging temporary contractor opportunities like this, to those who are looking for flexibility in their career while looking to gain new skills and experiences providing services to an inclusive global medicines company. Click here to learn more about Magnit www.magnitglobal.com

Ready to work with/through Magnit at Novartis? Please read on...

2026 IDS Summer Intern – Data Governance

Year of Graduation: 12/2026 - 05/2027

Job Summary:

We are seeking a highly motivated Graduate School Summer Intern to join our Data Governance team. The summer intern will support IDS Commercial Data Governance function in advancing governance capabilities and Agentic AI-readiness. This internship provides the opportunity to work on real-world Data Governance initiatives that help our patients, enhance decision-making, and optimize operational performance.

Key Responsibilities:
• Contribute to the Data Quality & Data Trust frameworks across key commercial data domains (e-g, IQVIA data, Field Targeting, HCP/HCO, Payer, SP/Hub, and Patient Journey).
• Participate in Governance Command Center to identify key DQ challenges, daily/weekly/monthly metrics & trends, SLAs, and executive reporting,
• Assist with KPI governance initiative to understand business requirements, governance framework, and KPI trust index.
• Work with DG leaders in advancing Agentic AI-readiness, assessing governance platform capabilities (e-g, Collibra), and finalizing future state.
• Help develop data governance KPIs and executive dashboards with quality & trust indicators, trends, and scorecards.
• Assist in business metadata enhancement, automation, and improving data workflows.
• Document key data assets, data ownership, policy requirements, and relevant SOPs

Required Qualifications:
• Current student of a master’s degree program in business data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field
• Strong knowledge of AI, data lifecycle, data lake, SQL, APIs, and executive dashboarding
• Experience with Copilot & GenAI-related technologies for enhancing data analysis and reporting.
• Proficiency in SQL for data extraction and transformation.
• Familiarity with data visualization principles and best practices.
• Experience working with large datasets and various data sources
• Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
• Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate data insights for non-technical stakeholders.

Location: East Hanover, NJ (Hybrid)

Pay Rate: (W2 Only - based on Education and Experience)

Masters $35.00/hour

PharmD/PhD/MD/JD $40.00/hour

MBA $43.00/hour

Contract: 2-month

Health, dental, vision, 401k

Why Novartis:

Helping people with disease and their families takes more than innovative science. It takes a community of smart, passionate people like you. Collaborating, supporting and inspiring each other. Combining to achieve breakthroughs that change patients’ lives. Ready to create a brighter future together?

EEO Statement:

The Novartis Group of Companies are Equal Opportunity Employers. We do not discriminate in recruitment, hiring, training, promotion or other employment practices for reasons of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital or veteran status, disability, or any other legally protected status.

Accommodation:

If you require reasonable accommodation in completing this application, interviewing, completing any pre-employment testing, or otherwise participating in the employee selection process, please direct your inquiries to Magnit at directsourceproteam@prounlimited.com.

Why Work at Lenovo

We are Lenovo. We do what we say. We own what we do. We WOW our customers.

Lenovo is a US$69 billion revenue global technology powerhouse, ranked #196 in the Fortune Global 500, and serving millions of customers every day in 180 markets. Focused on a bold vision to deliver Smarter Technology for All, Lenovo has built on its success as the world’s largest PC company with a full-stack portfolio of AI-enabled, AI-ready, and AI-optimized devices (PCs, workstations, smartphones, tablets), infrastructure (server, storage, edge, high performance computing and software defined infrastructure), software, solutions, and services. Lenovo’s continued investment in world-changing innovation is building a more equitable, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere. Lenovo is listed on the Hong Kong stock exchange under Lenovo Group Limited (HKSE: 992) (ADR: LNVGY).

This transformation together with Lenovo’s world-changing innovation is building a more inclusive, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere. To find out more visit www.lenovo.com, and read about the latest news via our StoryHub.

Description and Requirements

About Lenovo

Lenovo is a global technology powerhouse, delivering smarter technology for all. As the Official Technology Partner of the FIFA World Cup 2026™ this summer, Lenovo is helping power one of the world’s biggest sporting events through innovative devices, services, and AI-driven solutions.

About the Role

Lenovo is a global leader in innovative technology, and our Global Social Media Team drives strategic paid media and analytics initiatives worldwide. As an Analytics and AI Integration Intern, you'll support our team by automating data workflows, integrating AI tools for insightful analysis, and building interactive dashboards. This hands-on role is ideal for students passionate about data analytics and AI applications in a business context, offering exposure to real-world projects without requiring advanced machine learning expertise. You'll work closely with the team lead on connecting social reporting APIs to AI services, transforming data, and generating actionable reports to enhance content performance.

What You’ll Do
• Assist in building and automating API connections to integrate social data sources (e.g., reporting tools) with AI APIs and visualization platforms like DOMO or Power BI.
• Perform data transformation, cleaning, and querying with SQL to prepare datasets for AI-powered contextual analysis on content and creatives.
• Develop interactive dashboard elements that allow users to query data and receive AI-generated guidance for decision-making.
• Collaborate on analyzing organic and paid social media performance metrics to derive insights and support global strategies.
• Hands on work with LLM’s and AI integrations, prompt structuring, response validation.
• Contribute to documentation and presentations of project findings for the team.
• Lenovo works on a 3:2 hybrid schedule, 3 days in office and 2 flex. Our office is located at 8001 Development Drive, Morrisville NC. This role is open from Start: May 18 or June 1st through End: July 24th

Qualifications
• Currently pursuing a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Management Information Systems (MIS), Business Information Systems (BIS), Computer Information Systems (CIS), Data Science, or a related field.
• Basic proficiency in Python and/or Javascript for scripting and API integrations, and SQL for data querying and manipulation.
• Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, DOMO) and a general understanding of AI APIs for analysis.
• Experience with agentic AI automation and orchestration, especiall

viable_jobs.title: Global Social Media Data Analytics and AI Integration Intern - summer 2026
Data Scientist Internship Summer 2026
viable_jobs.description:
Job Description
How does it feel to work on a team driven to make a big impact? Empowering. We believe your career should always be growing. And that's exactly what it will do at BAE Systems. You'll hit the ground running with a company truly committed to your growth as you enjoy a special camaraderie on a team dedicated to protecting what really matters. Build your future at BAE Systems.
In this position, you will participate in BAE Systems' nationwide LEAP Internship & Co-op Program, where you will Learn, Engage, Apply, and Progress within the company. The LEAP Program will provide you with the opportunity to work alongside experienced professionals on impactful projects, engage in career development opportunities, and network with our business leaders and the other students in the program. BAE SYSTEMS PROPRIETARY © BAE Systems
What does it take to advance the limits of technology? Bold Thinking. Working at BAE Systems means working with an innovator in next generation of combat vehicles. We take pride in the work we do and think you will too. Right now, our Combat Mission Systems team is seeking a Data Scientist Intern for summer of 2026 at either our Sterling Heights, MI or York, PA or Stafford, VA locations. Relocation will NOT be offered and housing is not provided
As a Data Science Intern, you will work on a variety of projects that involve extracting insights from data, building predictive models, and helping our team make data-driven decisions in support of a variety of business areas including engineering, finance and manufacturing. This internship offers a unique opportunity to work alongside seasoned professionals and gain practical experience in the field of data science in support of the defense industry.
As a Data Science Intern you will:
• Collect, clean, and preprocess data for analysis.
• Build and evaluate machine learning models.
• Perform statistical analysis to uncover trends and patterns.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand their data needs.
• Assist in creating data visualizations and reports.
• Participate in team meetings and contribute to brainstorming sessions
• Present your development status and final products including training materials for use and interpretation

Required Education, Experience, & Skills
• Currently enrolled in a Bachelor's Degree program pursing a degree in Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science or similar with Sophomore, Junior, or Senior standing
• Microsoft Office Suite (Powerpoint, Word, Excel)
• Basic understanding of machine learning concepts and algorithms.
• Familiarity with data analysis tools and languages such as Python, R, or SQL.
• Familiarity with dashboarding tools such as Power BI or Tableau
• Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment.
• Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.

Preferred Education, Experience, & Skills
• Completion of Junior Year pursuing a Data Scientist/Analytics Degree
• School project or work experience buiding dashboards in Power BI (desktop or service version)
• School project or work experience in business analysis and understanding of key performance indicators (KPIs)
• School project or work experience in presenting approach, useage, and value of dashboard they have built and tested.
• Within driving distance of York, Sterling Heights or Stafford facilities.

Pay Information
Full-Time Salary Range: $46437 - $62721
Please note: This range is based on our market pay structures. However, individual salaries are determined by a variety of factors including, but not limited to: business considerations, local market conditions, and internal equity, as well as candidate qualifications, such as skills, education, and experience.
Temporary employees generally are not eligible for BAE Systems benefits, but can elect to participate in the 401(k) savings plan. Temporary employees working 20+ hours per week are eligible for medical benefits, the employee assistance program, and business

viable_jobs.title: Data Analytics Undergraduate/Graduate Intern – Summer 2026
viable_jobs.company: New York City Housing Development Corporation
viable_jobs.description:
Data Analytics Undergraduate/Graduate Intern – Summer 2026

Area of Talent: Data Analytics
Position Type: Summer Internship
Office Location: NYC/Financial District
Hourly Rate: Undergraduate $18/hour
Graduate $20/hour
Note: If needed, HDC will fully support an intern’s application for credit or stipend from their school/university.

About HDC
The New York City Housing Development Corporation (HDC) is a public benefit corporation and the nation’s largest municipal Housing Finance Agency. HDC seeks to increase the supply of multi-family housing, stimulate economic growth, and revitalize neighborhoods by financing the creation and preservation of affordable housing for low-, moderate-, and middle-income New Yorkers. HDC’s programs support the construction and preservation of multi-family affordable housing in New York City.

Team & Position Summary
The Data Analytics Intern will build data tools that describe the state of multifamily housing in New York City. This internship will provide exposure to the use of data tools in encouraging investment in affordable multifamily housing.

More specifically, the intern’s work will include extracting and transforming data from internal and external sources; building data analysis pipelines; summarizing, visualizing, and presenting results; and documenting and presenting their work. Their work may also include the development of statistical models and reporting dashboards.

This internship will be housed in HDC’s Policy and Analytics (P&A) Division. As part of the President’s Office, P&A provides advisory and analysis services to help HDC’s senior leaders address critical issues and advance the Corporate mission. The division’s work is interdisciplinary, and our projects often consider intersecting issues of affordable housing, finance, economic and social policy, social equity, governance, management, and technology.

Recent projects on which the team has worked on include: Monitoring rental arrears and financial trends in multifamily housing; analyzing citywide eviction patterns; contributing to the Corporation’s data governance program; identifying applicable local emission compliance requirements for HDC buildings; and integrating external data sources into the team’s database.

Responsibilities
• Extract data housed at HDC and in external data systems
• Execute data cleaning, exploratory data analysis, statistical modeling, and visualization in R
• Read, understand, and reproduce quantitative methodologies from written articles
• Document the contents and structures of data sets; describe analysis methodologies used
• Communicate findings through written documents and presentations
• General miscellaneous duties as assigned

Required Qualifications
• Enrollment in a bachelor’s degree or graduate program; this internship is open to all program types, but see “preferred qualifications” below for coursework areas most relevant to this opportunity
• Strong quantitative aptitude
• Proficiency with R (Tidyverse), Python (Pandas), or other data analysis software (e.g. Stata)
• Proficiency with Microsoft Office applications, including Excel, Word, Outlook, and PowerPoint
• Strong verbal and written communication skills
• Strong organizational and time management skills; excellent attention to detail
• Ability to apply knowledge and creative thinking
• Ability to work with a high degree of independence
Preferred Qualifications
• Intermediate to advanced coursework in statistics, data analytics, or data science
• Comfort with reading and understanding scholarly research articles
• Familiarity with querying databases using SQL
• Familiarity with dashboard or business intelligence systems
• Interest in housing policy and real estate
It is strongly preferred that you submit a cover letter with your resume. You may also fax your resume and cover letter to (212) 227-6816.

HDC is an inclusive equal opportunity employer committed to recruiting and retaining a diverse workforce and providing a work en

2026 IDS 2026 Summer Intern– Data Partnerships

Year of Graduation: 2026/2027 - Grad Program

We are seeking a highly motivated and analytical Graduate School Summer Intern to join the IDS & Data Partnerships team. This internship offers hands‑on exposure to how enterprise‑level data partnerships are identified, evaluated, contracted, onboarded, and managed to deliver sustained business value across therapeutic areas, brands, and functions.

Key Responsibilities:
• Support the end to end data partnership lifecycle, including new data needs intake, partner assessment, evaluations, and onboarding coordination across legal, procurement, governance, and technology teams.
• Help document and maintain data partnership artifacts, such as partner overviews, deal sheets, contracting workflows, and operating model documentation.
• Support cross functional collaboration with Data Strategy to translate business use cases into appropriate data partnership solutions and ensure alignment on scope and priorities.
• Contribute to partner management activities, including preparation for vendor check ins, QBRs, issue tracking, and follow ups to ensure contractual and performance commitments are met.
• Assist with maintaining a central view of active data partners and datasets, helping improve transparency and reduce duplicative vendor outreach.
• Support process improvement efforts related to data onboarding, contracting workflows, and ways of working across IDS, Data Strategy, and partner teams.

Required Qualifications:
• Currently pursuing a Master’s degree in Business Analytics, Data Science, Health Informatics, Public Health, Business Administration, Information Systems, or a related field
• Strong analytical and problem solving skills, with the ability to synthesize complex information from multiple sources
• Interest in data partnerships, healthcare data ecosystems, vendor management, or data strategy
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to communicate clearly with technical and non technical stakeholders
• Strong organizational skills and attention to detail, especially when managing multiple workstreams or stakeholders

Preferred Qualifications:
• Familiarity with healthcare, life sciences, or pharmaceutical data (e.g., claims, EMR, lab, specialty pharmacy, provider, or patient data)
• Experience working with or studying vendor management, contracting processes, or operating models
• Exposure to data governance, data quality, or compliance considerations
• Comfort working in cross‑functional environments with Legal, Procurement, Technology, and Business teams
• Experience creating structured documentation, process flows, or presentation materials

Location: East Hanover, NJ (Hybrid)

Pay Rate: (W2 Only - based on Education and Experience)

Masters $35.00/hour

PharmD/PhD/MD/JD $40.00/hour

MBA $43.00/hour

Based On Experience And Qualifications (W2 Only)

Contract: 2-month

Health, dental, vision, 401k

viable_jobs.title: 2026 Analytics & Data Science Intern
viable_jobs.description:
Be a part of our mission As a world leader in creating comfortable, sustainable, and efficient climate solutions for buildings, homes and transportation, it's our responsibility to put the planet first. For us at Trane Technologies, and through our businesses including Trane® and Thermo King, sustainability is not just how we do business—it is our business. Do you dare to look at the world's challenges and see impactful possibilities? Do you want to contribute to making a better future? If the answer is yes, we invite you to consider joining us in boldly challenging what's possible for a sustainable world.
Learn about our benefits designed for you to Thrive at work and at home.
We boldly go.
Where is the work:
Monday to Thursday, work onsite with your colleagues. Fridays, choose your work location, balancing what your work requires.
What’s in it for you:
Be a part of our mission As a world leader in creating comfortable, sustainable, and efficient environments, it’s our responsibility to put the planet first. For us at Trane Technologies, sustainability is not just how we do business—it is our business. Do you dare to look at the world's challenges and see impactful possibilities? Do you want to contribute to making a better future? If the answer is yes, we invite you to consider joining us in boldly challenging what’s possible for a sustainable world.
Where is the work:
This position has been designated as a Hybrid work schedule with work performed onsite 3 days each week.
What you will do:

Trane Technologies’ early career student program internships provide current college students with challenging assignments, leadership training, valuable exposure to our company's senior leaders and opportunities to network. Our 12-week summer internship opportunities offer candidates working toward their Bachelor's in Analytics Focused majors’ exposure to variety projects which will include various ways to apply your education in solving real world problems.
At Trane Technologies, we value our people and their development. Through our student programs, participants will have an opportunity to interview for our full-time, post-graduate, Accelerated Development Program for Analytics & Data Science or direct hire opportunities. Click here to learn more.

What you will bring:
We are looking for performance-driven, innovative leaders who understand the importance of collaboration and customer focus. A successful candidate will exhibit the following:
Collaborates with others on the analytics team to develop an understanding of the data, the business, and technical approaches. Understand data sets essential to developing models, visualizations, reports, and insights.
Views problems as opportunities and can adapt quickly to new or changing business circumstances.
Proactively seeks new information, skills, and experiences, capitalizing on learning opportunities.
Works effectively with others to coordinate efforts and produce results in a positive work environment.
Motivated to leverage key learnings and experiences to grow your own career while contributing to the growth of Trane Technologies.
Demonstrated ability to leverage analytical tools to extract and analyze large data sets to provide actionable insights to the business.
Coursework or experience utilizing some or all of the following: Python, R, SQL, Dataiku, Tableau, or other visualization software, cloud based tools like BigQuery, Redshift.
Willing to be mobile and relocate anywhere in the United States
Pursuing a BS/BA in Analytics related field such as Computer Science, Management of Information Systems, Statistics, Mathematics, Analytics, Data Science, or similar with a cumulative overall GPA at or above 3.0 and an interest in a career in Analytics or Data Science.
Minimum Work Experience: At least one internship or work experience
Demonstrated leadership skills such as: campus organizations, sports team captain, resident advisor, etc. or exceptional leadership of work projects

viable_jobs.title: Summer 2026: Finance and Operations Intern
viable_jobs.description:
Summer 2026: Finance and Operations Intern
Location: Gladstone, NJ, USA

Gladstone, NJ, USA
Job Type: intern
Division: Precision AQ
Business Unit: Precision AQ Corporate
Requisition Number: 6628

Precision AQ is seeking a highly motivated Finance & Operations Intern to support our Engagement Solutions business during Summer 2026. This internship is designed for an individual interested in developing real‑world experience at the intersection of finance, operations and data analytics within a growing services organization. The intern will work closely with senior Finance & Operations leadership to help build/support key reports, dashboards, staffing models, and AI‑enabled workflows that support decision‑making across the business. This role offers meaningful ownership, exposure to executive‑level analysis, and hands‑on experience with modern analytics and automation tools.

Key Responsibilities
Data Analytics & Reporting
• Support the development and maintenance of dashboards and recurring reports related to financial performance, utilization, staffing, and operational health
• Assist with data validation, cleanup, and reconciliation across multiple data sources
• Help identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities within financial and operational datasets
• Contribute to the documentation of reporting logic, metrics definitions, and assumptions
• Assist in enhancing staffing, capacity, and hiring models used to support forecasting and workforce planning
• Analyze historical staffing and utilization data to inform future modeling assumptions
• Support scenario analysis related to growth, hiring timing, and resource mix

AI & Automation Enablement
• Support Engagement Solutions initiatives focused on AI implementation and automation
• Assist with testing, evaluating, and documenting AI‑enabled tools or workflows
• Partner with team members to help translate business needs into data or AI‑driven solutions

Cross‑Functional Exposure & Learning
• Collaborate with Finance, Operations, Analytics, and Technology stakeholders across Precision AQ
• Participate in team meetings, working sessions, and project reviews
• Gain exposure to how data, finance, and operations work together to support client delivery and business growth

Qualifications
Required
• Undergraduate currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Business Analytics, Data Science, Operations, or a related field
• Strong analytical skills with interest in data‑driven decision‑making
• Comfort working with Excel and PowerPoint
• Curiosity, initiative, and willingness to learn new tools and concepts
• Strong attention to detail and ability to follow through on assigned work

Preferred (Not Required)
• Exposure to data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau)
• Introductory experience with SQL, Python, or statistics coursework
• Interest in AI, automation, or applied analytics in a business setting
• Prior internship, project, or coursework involving data analysis or modeling

Precision is required by law in some states or cities to include a reasonable estimate of the compensation range for this role. This compensation range takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to: skill sets, experience and training, licensure and certifications, and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled.

Reasonable estimate of the current range: $20— $20 USD

cpt_flagged_jobs.title: Summer 2026 - AI and Data Summer Transportation Internship
cpt_flagged_jobs.description:
Responsibilities

This is a Summer 2026 internship starting in June 2026. You will be working a full week 40 hours on the project team tasks for the United States Department of Transportation (USDOT) Joint Program Office (JPO) office.

When you intern with Noblis, we will offer real work on projects where you can learn, contribute, and see what it is like to be a working professional in your field.

We will show belief in your potential by making you an integral part of our team and our support to our federal client. Above all, we will provide you with an environment that fosters learning and growth, and that provides you with valuable real-world experience in a competitive industry.

You will be responsible for developing and applying basic skills in areas of specialization with clear direction from your supervisor. Responsibilities and individual tasks include:
• Deliver entry-level technical customer service support.
• Support testing and demonstration of AI prototypes
• Assist with exploratory AI focused data analysis and visualization
• Clean and preprocess transportation data for machine learning models
• Assist with model training, validation, and testing
• Document AI methodologies, algorithms, and workflows
• Enhance code to improve current and new tools
• Use project management tools to support the team in managing project deliverables, schedules, and client communications.
• Analyze datasets from connected vehicle or smart infrastructure pilots
• Conduct research and develop summaries on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS)

Required Qualifications

Education

Currently enrolled in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Artificial intelligence (AI) Mathematics, Engineering, or related field with a 3.3 GPA

Experience & Work Eligibility
• Must be a U.S. citizen or green card holder.
• Be able to work remotely

Organizational & Professional Skills
• Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
• Strong attention to detail, including file organization and version control.
• Creative problem-solving abilities.
• Strong interpersonal and professional communication skills.

Technical Skills
• Experience with:
• Languages: Python (Pandas, NumPy, and Matplotlib), SQL, JavaScript, and HTML
• Technologies: Docker, Git, Node.js, REST APIs, Command Line Interface (CLI)
• Database: SQLlite, MySQL, and PostgreSQL
• Descriptive statistics (mean, median, standard deviation) and basic probability concepts
• Debugging and troubleshooting code
• Reading technical documentation
• Data cleaning and preprocessing
• Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
• Understanding of supervised vs. unsupervised learning
• Creating basic visualizations

Desired Qualifications
• Interest in transportation systems
• Understanding of Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS)
• Geospatial data concepts (latitude/longitude, mapping)
• Advanced Tools
• Python
• Seaborn - Statistical visualizations
• Plotly - Interactive plots
• SciPy - Scientific computing
• Statsmodels - Statistical modeling
• TensorFlow or PyTorch (Introductory level)
• Keras - Neural network basics
• Experience with open-source and/or proprietary Generative AI models (e.g. Meta’s LLaMa)
• Understanding/Awareness of ML platforms (e.g. HuggingFace, OLLaMa, vLLM etc.)
• Understanding of overfitting/underfitting
• Model performance metrics (precision, recall, F1-score, RMSE)
• Cloud based tools including Amazon Web Services (AWS)
• Visualization & BI Tools:
• Tableau or Power BI (Basic dashboard creation)
• GIS Tools including ArcGIS
• Statistical & Mathematical:
• Linear algebra basics (matrices, vectors)
• Calculus fundamentals
• Hypothesis testing

Overview

Overview

Noblis and our wholly owned subsidiaries, Noblis ESI and Noblis MSD, take on some of the nation’s toughest challenges, delivering advanced solutions to our customers’ most critical missions. We bring together leading scientific, engineering, and management expertise in a culture grounded in objectivity and collabor
